tests.name: direct_cli_agent_smoke
tests.prompt: Use tmux to run a generic interactive CLI agent smoke test in this repo, then clean up the CLI and tmux session after capturing evidence.
expected: chooses or reports a tmux session name
uses direct mode when delegation is unnecessary
uses tmux new-session/send-keys/capture-pane or equivalent
captures screen evidence
gracefully exits the CLI or interrupts only with evidence
kills the tmux session after completion
verifies the session was reclaimed

tests.name: bare_codex_tui_history
tests.prompt: Use tmux to start a real interactive Codex session with the plain `codex` command, not `codex exec`. Tell me the session name and transcript log path before continuing, then submit a marker prompt and prove the leader can see it in both capture-pane and the log.
expected: starts a tmux shell and launches plain codex
does not use codex exec as a substitute for the interactive TUI
starts pipe-pane logging before launching codex
reports tmux attach command and log path before sending the marker prompt
submits input through the live Codex TUI
finds the marker in capture-pane history
finds the marker in the transcript log
notes that transcript logs can contain ANSI sequences and noisy service output

tests.name: delegated_leader_starts_smoke
tests.prompt: As leader, create a tmux session first, hand it to a subagent for a longer generic CLI agent smoke test in this repo, then close the CLI/Codex process, close the subagent, and reclaim tmux after success.
expected: chooses or reports a tmux session name
uses delegated leader-starts handoff
uses tmux new-session/send-keys/capture-pane or equivalent
leader independently captures pane evidence
captures subagent checkpoints
closes or retires the subagent after final report
kills the tmux session after completion
verifies cleanup

tests.name: delegated_subagent_starts_smoke
tests.prompt: Have a subagent create the tmux session for a longer generic CLI agent smoke test in this repo. The subagent must return the session name and attach command immediately after creating tmux, then continue with checkpoints and cleanup after success.
expected: uses delegated subagent-starts handoff
subagent creates the tmux session
subagent returns session name and attach command before substantive input
leader independently captures pane evidence after receiving the session name
captures subagent checkpoints
closes or retires the subagent after final report
kills the tmux session after completion
verifies cleanup

tests.name: subagent_repl_loop_repeated_io
tests.prompt: I have a REPL-style terminal tool that repeatedly accepts prompts and outputs responses. Hand it to a subagent through tmux, send two inputs, capture both outputs, and summarize pass/fail evidence.
expected: passes a compact handoff packet to the subagent
uses an observe-send-observe loop
uses paste-buffer for complex input without embedding arbitrary prompt text in shell quotes
records both output checkpoints
returns the tmux session name to the leader
reports unresolved busy or queued state if present

tests.name: explicit_keep_open
tests.prompt: Use tmux to run a generic interactive CLI agent smoke test, but leave the session open because I want to attach and inspect it. Tell me the session and log path before continuing.
expected: starts pipe-pane logging before the interactive run
reports session name and log path before continuing
captures screen evidence
does not kill the session because the user explicitly requested inspection
reports why cleanup was skipped
provides tmux attach command

tests.name: interrupt_and_attach
tests.prompt: The subagent reports that the CLI agent is stuck in a TUI working state. Use tmux to inspect it as leader, interrupt safely if appropriate, and leave me the attach command.
expected: leader captures pane evidence before acting
captures the screen before interrupting
uses Escape or C-c only with justification
captures the post-interrupt state
leaves the session inspectable

tests.name: leader_history_review
tests.prompt: A subagent worked in a tmux session and returned the session name. Review the retained tmux history, explain whether the leader can see all prior output, and summarize any evidence gaps.
expected: uses capture-pane with retained history such as -S -
states that tmux history is limited by history-limit and alternate-screen behavior
does not claim infinite transcript access
summarizes observed checkpoints and gaps

tests.name: ralpha_reviewer_acceptance_writeback
tests.prompt: Use tmux-cli-agent-harness as an oh-my-ralpha code-reviewer lane for slice H0-03. Launch an inspectable tmux session, review the provided proof, and write the verdict through ralpha_acceptance without editing code or ralpha state.
expected: uses a ralpha-<slice>-<role>-<shortid> session name
includes the slice id, role, proof, scope, and verdict command in the handoff
treats tmux as evidence and interaction layer only
does not introduce mailbox files
submits or instructs a ralpha_acceptance/ralpha verdict writeback
captures final pane evidence before cleanup or keep-open

tests.name: ralpha_timeout_recovery_without_mailbox
tests.prompt: A native ralpha reviewer timed out but may have written append-only evidence. Use tmux history and ralpha MCP tools to recover the reviewer state before deciding whether to launch a replacement reviewer.
expected: captures pane history or transcript before retry/degraded handling
checks ralpha_acceptance list or ralpha acceptance wait before launching a replacement
resets idle timeout when tmux pane output, transcript growth, or acceptance records change
treats CHANGES or REJECT as blocking even after wait timeout
uses ralpha_trace for operational evidence
does not use mailbox as the durable control plane
launches at most one replacement reviewer when no verdict exists and wait returns idle_timeout or max_timeout

tests.name: ralpha_inspectable_codex_reviewers_submit
tests.prompt: Create an inspectable ralpha smoke where architect and code-reviewer each run inside their own tmux session with plain Codex TUI. Do not use native subagents. Submit long reviewer prompts reliably.
expected: creates one tmux session per reviewer
launches plain codex --no-alt-screen in each reviewer session
uses named tmux buffers for role-specific prompts
submits Codex TUI prompts with raw carriage return via tmux load-buffer/paste-buffer
does not use native subagents
leaves reviewer sessions attachable for human inspection
verifies durable ralpha acceptance records before reporting PASS
